definitions.usage_example: [TRADUCIR AL FR: Although it was cold, I went out.]
definitions.pronunciation_guide_es: [PRONUNCIACIÓN EN FR PARA HABLANTES DE EN]
definitions.usage_example_es: Although it was cold, I went out.
definitions.usage_context_en: In spite of the fact that; even though.
definitions.usage_context_es: [TRADUCIR AL FR: In spite of the fact that; even though.]

definitions.usage_example: [TRADUCIR AL FR: Though it was cold, I went out.]
definitions.pronunciation_guide_es: [PRONUNCIACIÓN EN FR PARA HABLANTES DE EN]
definitions.usage_example_es: Though it was cold, I went out.
definitions.usage_context_es: [TRADUCIR AL FR: A less formal version of 'although'.]

definitions.usage_example: [TRADUCIR AL FR: He is rich. He is not happy, though.]
definitions.pronunciation_guide_es: [PRONUNCIACIÓN EN FR PARA HABLANTES DE EN]
definitions.usage_example_es: He is rich. He is not happy, though.
definitions.usage_context_en: Used as an adverb at the end of a sentence, meaning 'however'.
definitions.usage_context_es: [TRADUCIR AL FR: Used as an adverb at the end of a sentence, meaning 'however'.]

definitions.usage_example: [TRADUCIR AL FR: Even though it was cold, I went out.]
definitions.pronunciation_guide_es: [PRONUNCIACIÓN EN FR PARA HABLANTES DE EN]
definitions.usage_example_es: Even though it was cold, I went out.
definitions.usage_context_en: A stronger, more emphatic version of 'although'.
definitions.usage_context_es: [TRADUCIR AL FR: A stronger, more emphatic version of 'although'.]

name: [TRADUCIR AL FR: on the other hand]

definitions.usage_example: [TRADUCIR AL FR: This car is cheap. On the other hand, it is very old.]
definitions.pronunciation_guide_es: [PRONUNCIACIÓN EN FR PARA HABLANTES DE EN]
definitions.usage_example_es: This car is cheap. On the other hand, it is very old.
definitions.usage_context_en: Used to introduce a contrasting point of view or fact.
definitions.usage_context_es: [TRADUCIR AL FR: Used to introduce a contrasting point of view or fact.]

definitions.usage_example: [TRADUCIR AL FR: He is rich. However, he is not happy.]
definitions.pronunciation_guide_es: [PRONUNCIACIÓN EN FR PARA HABLANTES DE EN]
definitions.usage_example_es: He is rich. However, he is not happy.
definitions.usage_context_en: Used to introduce a statement that contrasts with something said previously.
definitions.usage_context_es: [TRADUCIR AL FR: Used to introduce a statement that contrasts with something said previously.]

definitions.usage_example: [TRADUCIR AL FR: However you look at it, it's a bad situation.]
definitions.pronunciation_guide_es: [PRONUNCIACIÓN EN FR PARA HABLANTES DE EN]
definitions.usage_example_es: However you look at it, it's a bad situation.
definitions.usage_context_es: [TRADUCIR AL FR: In whatever way or manner.]

definitions.usage_example: [TRADUCIR AL FR: He likes coffee, while I prefer tea.]
definitions.pronunciation_guide_es: [PRONUNCIACIÓN EN FR PARA HABLANTES DE EN]
definitions.usage_example_es: He likes coffee, while I prefer tea.
definitions.usage_context_en: Used to contrast two different facts or situations.
definitions.usage_context_es: [TRADUCIR AL FR: Used to contrast two different facts or situations.]

definitions.usage_example: [TRADUCIR AL FR: I read while I wait.]
definitions.pronunciation_guide_es: [PRONUNCIACIÓN EN FR PARA HABLANTES DE EN]
definitions.usage_context_en: During the time that; at the same time as.
definitions.usage_context_es: [TRADUCIR AL FR: During the time that; at the same time as.]

definitions.usage_example: [TRADUCIR AL FR: I will stay home, unless it stops raining.]
definitions.pronunciation_guide_es: [PRONUNCIACIÓN EN FR PARA HABLANTES DE EN]
definitions.usage_example_es: I will stay home, unless it stops raining.
definitions.usage_context_en: Used to introduce a negative condition (same as 'if... not').
definitions.usage_context_es: [TRADUCIR AL FR: Used to introduce a negative condition (same as 'if... not').]

definitions.usage_example: [TRADUCIR AL FR: You can go, as long as you finish your homework.]
definitions.pronunciation_guide_es: [PRONUNCIACIÓN EN FR PARA HABLANTES DE EN]
definitions.usage_example_es: You can go, as long as you finish your homework.
definitions.usage_context_en: Used to introduce a condition that must be true for the main clause to be true.
definitions.usage_context_es: [TRADUCIR AL FR: Used to introduce a condition that must be true for the main clause to be true.]

definitions.usage_example: [TRADUCIR AL FR: You need to study hard; otherwise, you will fail.]
definitions.pronunciation_guide_es: [PRONUNCIACIÓN EN FR PARA HABLANTES DE EN]
definitions.usage_example_es: You need to study hard; otherwise, you will fail.
definitions.usage_context_en: In circumstances different from those present or considered.
definitions.usage_context_es: [TRADUCIR AL FR: In circumstances different from those present or considered.]
